channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra wins the top spot in November's race for California governor
item.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has clinched the top spot on California's ballot for governor. With millions of ballots still to be counted, his November challenger is unknown.
item.content:encoded:
Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has clinched the top spot on California's ballot for governor. With millions of ballots still to be counted, his November challenger is unknown.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Lagos

item.title: After D.C.'s Reflecting Pool gets repainted, visitors ask: What changed?
item.description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.
item.content:encoded:
The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Even America's 250th birthday has grown tumultuous
item.description: The United States is throwing a big 250th birthday party this summer. Planning between two groups has become highly politicized.
item.content:encoded: The United States is throwing a big 250th birthday party this summer. Planning between two groups has become highly politicized.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Staff told to remove President Trump from the Kennedy Center's name
item.description: President Trump's name is coming off the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's name is coming off the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Graham Platner isn't going anywhere in Maine Senate race after latest controversy
item.description: Graham Platner is denying accusations of being physically rough with former girlfriends saying that report in The New York Times and other controversies are a sign his campaign is gaining momentum.
item.content:encoded:
Graham Platner is denying accusations of being physically rough with former girlfriends saying that report in The New York Times and other controversies are a sign his campaign is gaining momentum.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: New World screwworm found in calf in Texas
item.description: A case of New World screwworm has been found in a calf in Texas. The flesh-eating fly, which was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s, poses a major threat to the cattle industry.
item.content:encoded: A case of New World screwworm has been found in a calf in Texas. The flesh-eating fly, which was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s, poses a major threat to the cattle industry.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
item.description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The quiz tracked Trump's wins and losses this week. Can you win bigly?
item.description: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.content:encoded: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: A global World Cup tradition catches fire in the U.S.: Panini sticker collecting
item.description: Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.
item.content:encoded:
Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: The Supreme Court has left limited alternatives for protecting minority voting rights
item.description: After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.
item.content:encoded:
After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang
